summary: Feature request to expose the private 'canvasSize' field as a public 'CanvasSize' property on SKCanvasView and SKGLView in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor, matching all other platform views which already expose it.

repoLinks.description: PR that exposed Dpi on Blazor views (analogous prior work)
PR by same author adding CanvasSize — closed without merge

fixStatus.reason: PR #1912 was submitted to add this property but was closed without merging on 2023-05-18. The canvasSize field remains private in current source code.

analysis.summary: Both SKCanvasView.razor.cs and SKGLView.razor.cs have a private 'SKSize canvasSize' field used internally for rendering but not exposed to consumers. Every other platform view (WPF, WinUI, WinForms, iOS, macOS, Android, Tizen) exposes a public 'CanvasSize' property. PR #1912 was submitted by the issue author to add 'public SKSize CanvasSize => canvasSize;' to both components but was closed without merge.
analysis.rationale: This is clearly a feature request, not a bug — the reporter explicitly uses the FEATURE template and references the analogous Dpi feature (#1831). The requested change is a one-line property addition per component, consistent with all other platform views. The fact that PR #1912 was closed without merge suggests the maintainer may have other plans or concerns, but the feature gap still exists.

keySignals.text: analogous to #1831 & #1832 it may is useful to expose the canvasSize Property, too
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter drew a direct parallel to the Dpi property that was exposed via PR #1832.

keySignals.text: Add a public property on both components to expose the private field.
keySignals.interpretation: Clear, specific ask — minimal change needed.

keySignals.text: anyone who wants to do non-trivial JS interop with this canvas element needs the htmlCanvas ElementReference exposed
keySignals.interpretation: Commenter goes further, also requesting ElementReference exposure for JS interop.

codeInvestigation.finding: Private field 'SKSize canvasSize' exists but has no public property — all other platform views expose CanvasSize publicly.
Same pattern — private 'SKSize canvasSize' field with no public accessor.
WPF exposes 'public SKSize CanvasSize { get; private set; }' — confirms the standard pattern across other platforms.
Dpi is already exposed as 'public double Dpi => dpi;' via PR #1832, establishing the precedent for this same pattern.

resolution.hypothesis: The canvasSize field is intentionally private only because it was not exposed during initial Blazor component development. PR #1832 established the pattern by exposing Dpi; CanvasSize should follow.

proposals.title: Add public CanvasSize property to both Blazor views
proposals.description: Add 'public SKSize CanvasSize => canvasSize;' to both SKCanvasView.razor.cs and SKGLView.razor.cs, matching the pattern used for the Dpi property and consistent with all other platform views.

proposals.title: Use SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs.Info.Size inside OnPaintSurface callback
proposals.description: As a workaround, access the canvas size from the Info property of SKPaintSurfaceEventArgs within the OnPaintSurface callback. This provides the user-visible size but is only accessible during painting.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add public CanvasSize property to both Blazor views
resolution.recommendedReason: Trivial one-line change per file, consistent with all other platform views and the Dpi property already on these same components.

actionability.reason: Valid feature request for a trivial API gap. The property exists on every other platform view. PR #1912 was closed without merge, but the gap remains. This should be kept open for implementation.

actions.description: Apply feature-request, Blazor views, and WASM labels
Acknowledge the request and provide workaround
Link to closed PR #1912 that attempted this fix
